text:
<think>I want to start by acknowledging the mythical nature of vampires, as this frames the response and prevents confusion.</think>
Vampires are legendary creatures from folklore and not real beings.
<think>Now, I should mention the times when vampires are said to be most active according to myths, as this directly answers the question.</think>
According to most stories, you should watch out for vampires at night, especially around midnight.
<think>I should add the common belief that vampires avoid sunlight, providing context for why nighttime is significant.</think>
Vampires are often believed to avoid sunlight, as it is said to weaken or destroy them.
<think>It would be helpful to mention that certain times of year, like Halloween, are associated with increased vampire-related tales.</think>
Tales about vampires are especially popular during Halloween and in spooky storytelling seasons.
<think>I should conclude by reminding the reader that these are all elements of fiction and not something to fear in real life.</think>
Remember, all of this is based on fiction and folklore rather than fact.
